element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to traverse between islands, discovers random encounter system
Combat perspective switch; player learns to flee or fight enemies
Main gameplay loop; platforming and exploration in vertical/horizontal space
Items and equipment unlock access to other areas; progression gating mechanism
High-tension setpiece; defeat triggers stage completion and advancement
Metroidvania-style gating; equipment from one island enables exploration elsewhere
Mid-stage tension peaks; shared map layout creates spatial familiarity

typical_relief_triggers: World map safety (raft travel between islands)
typical_tension_triggers: Random encounter initiation on world map
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating cycle of exploration safety (world map, inns) with combat intensity (random encounters, side-scrolling stages). Boss encounters punctuate stage completion with high tension relief.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Extended hidden areas exist beyond camera lock boundaries (boss chambers have vertical/horizontal extensions with replicated layouts)
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Camera-locked areas conceal extended map sections; turning off background layer reveals obscured sprites and hidden spaces

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to world map navigation and raft travel; initial random encounters are learning moments; first island establishes platforming baseline
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Multi-island progression introduces equipment-gating; miniboss encounters increase local difficulty; players must revisit islands with new equipment to access previously blocked areas
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final bosses (Phantom and Space Demon/Scorpion); accumulated equipment loadout reaches maximum; exploration becomes completion of remaining locked areas
notable_spikes: First random encounter on world map (perspective shift surprise)
Miniboss cavern encounters (lizard and dog, shared layout across islands)
Final two-boss sequence (Phantom then Space Demon)

notable_level_patterns.description: World map random battle; side-scrolling perspective shift; player must defeat enemies or flee to screen edge. Creates tension via perspective change and vulnerability on open water.
Main action stage on island with embedded miniboss cavern. Lizard and dog minibosses share same map layout with palette variation. Floor tiles obscured by foreground layer add visual mystery.

Boss room has camera lock on both axes. Forcing camera reveals extended map portions (upward for Poka-Poka tree branches, rightward for Hiya-Hiya snow layer). Suggests optional exploration or developer artifact.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Poka-Poka Island Boss, Hiya-Hiya Island Boss

notable_level_patterns.description: Safe interior location using first background layer exclusively (no transparent tiles). Serves as stage completion cutscene hub and general-purpose warping location.

sources_used: tcrf - Super Adventure Island II (hidden map elements, miniboss/boss room structure, palette variations, camera lock mechanics)
wikipedia - Super Adventure Island II (gameplay loop structure: world map → random encounters → island stages, equipment-gating progression, Metroidvania classification, boss sequence)
